source_docs.title: Single-cell whole-genome analyses by Linear Amplification via Transposon Insertion (LIANTI)

oligos.name: LIANTI transposon
oligos.role: Self-looping Tn5 transposon carrying a single-stranded T7-promoter loop; dimerizes with Tn5 to form the LIANTI transposome

components.name: Tn5 mosaic end (non-transferred, 5' arm)
components.role: 19-bp transposase binding site; folds back to pair with the 3' arm

components.name: T7 promoter loop
components.role: single-stranded loop containing the T7 promoter (TAATACGACTCACTATAGGG)

components.name: Tn5 mosaic end (transferred, 3' arm)
components.role: 19-bp transposase binding site (mosaic end) transferred to genomic DNA

oligos.notes: Synthesized with a 5' phosphate (/5Phos/); PAGE-purified IDT oligo. Anneals into a 1.5 uM self-looping structure: the 5' and 3' 19-mers are reverse complements and form the 19-bp double-stranded transposase binding site, leaving the T7 promoter as a single-stranded loop. After transcription this loop is converted into a 19-bp sequence on the RNA 3' end that forms a 27-nt self-priming hairpin.

oligos.name: Second-strand synthesis primer (UMI primer)
oligos.role: Anneals to the 3' end of the first-strand cDNA and primes second-strand synthesis, appending an 8-bp unique molecular barcode (UMI) to each amplicon

components.name: UMI
components.role: 8-bp unique molecular identifier (8 x N), added to the 5' end of each LIANTI amplicon for digital counting / PCR-duplicate removal

components.name: T7 start + Tn5 mosaic end
components.role: binding sequence complementary to the 3' end of the cDNA (T7 +1 GGG followed by the 19-bp mosaic end)

oligos.notes: Written 5'-NNNNNNNNGGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-3' in the protocol; PAGE-purified IDT oligo. The 8 N's are the inline UMI trimmed off before mapping and used for de-duplication on the 5' end.

oligos.name: Carrier ssDNA
oligos.role: Carrier oligonucleotide added to the single-cell lysis buffer and to the in vitro transcription reaction to reduce loss of trace nucleic acids
oligos.notes: IDT oligo with standard desalting; used at 0.5 uM in lysis buffer and 0.4 uM in the T7 transcription reaction. Not part of the sequenceable library.

oligos.name: Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.role: Flow-cell P5 grafting sequence at the 5' end of the final library (added during NEBNext/TruSeq adapter ligation + indexing PCR)
oligos.notes: Canonical Illumina P5 sequence. Not printed in the protocol; library prep used the NEBNext Ultra / Ultra II DNA Library Prep Kit (TruSeq chemistry), so standard P5/P7/TruSeq oligos apply.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer
oligos.role: Read 1 sequencing primer; primes reading of the inline UMI + mosaic-end binding sequence + genomic insert
oligos.notes: Canonical TruSeq Read 1 sequence (5' ACAC overlaps the 3' end of P5). Standard NEBNext/TruSeq oligo; not printed in the protocol.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 2 primer
oligos.role: Read 2 sequencing primer; primes reading of the genomic insert from the opposite (sonicated) end
oligos.notes: Canonical TruSeq Read 2 sequence; also serves as the i7 index read primer. Standard NEBNext/TruSeq oligo; not printed in the protocol.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq adapter (double-stranded)
oligos.role: Y-shaped/forked adapter ligated to end-repaired, dA-tailed LIANTI amplicon fragments during NEBNext library prep

components.name: TruSeq adapter (top strand)
components.role: ligated forked adapter, top strand (Read 2 side)

components.name: TruSeq adapter (bottom strand)
components.role: ligated forked adapter, bottom strand (Read 1 side)

oligos.notes: Canonical TruSeq forked adapter. Standard NEBNext/TruSeq oligo; not printed in the protocol (kit-supplied).

oligos.name: Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.role: Flow-cell P7 grafting sequence at the 3' end of the final library; carried on the indexed PCR primer
oligos.notes: Canonical Illumina P7 sequence (appears reverse-complemented, ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG, at the 3' end of the top-strand library). NEBNext index PCR primer carries P7 + the i7 sample index. Standard oligo; not printed in the protocol.

oligos.name: NEBNext i7 index (sample index) primer
oligos.role: Indexed library PCR primer that appends the i7 sample index and P7; used to multiplex samples (e.g., 24 samples across HiSeq 4000 lanes)

components.name: TruSeq Read 2 primer binding
components.role: anneals to the ligated TruSeq adapter

oligos.notes: The protocol states 'NEBNext Indexes' were used (24 samples across 4 HiSeq 4000 lanes). NEBNext single-index i7 barcodes are 6 bp. Exact index sequences are not printed in the protocol.

final_library.source_label: LIANTI amplicon converted to a standard paired-end Illumina (NEBNext Ultra / TruSeq) genomic library
annotation_lines: TCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT = Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer (5' ACAC shared with P5)
GGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG = T7 +1 start (GGG) + Tn5 mosaic-end binding sequence
ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG = reverse complement of Illumina P7 adapter

evidence.locator: LIANTI amplicon converted to a standard paired-end Illumina (NEBNext Ultra / TruSeq) genomic library

segments.name: T7 start + Tn5 mosaic end (binding sequence)

library_generation.title: Single-cell lysis
library_generation.summary: A single cell is lysed and its genomic DNA released for amplification.
library_generation.note: A single BJ fibroblast is mouth-pipetted or FACS-sorted into lysis buffer (Tris-Ac, EDTA, DTT, carrier ssDNA), heated to 75 C, then digested with QIAGEN protease (55 C) and heat-inactivated, freeing intact single-cell genomic DNA (~6 pg).

library_generation.title: LIANTI transposome assembly
library_generation.summary: The self-looping LIANTI transposon is loaded onto Tn5 to form the transposome dimer.
library_generation.note: The single-stranded LIANTI transposon anneals into a self-loop whose 19-bp mosaic-end arms form a double-stranded transposase binding site, leaving a single-stranded T7-promoter loop. Equimolar transposon + Tn5 dimerize into the LIANTI transposome (two transposase monomers, two DNA pieces).
library_generation.product:
single-stranded T7 promoter loop
       ( ...TAATACGACTCACTATAGGG )
      /                          \
5'/Phos/CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT      AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-3'
        |||||||||||||||||||  (folds back / pairs)
        [ Tn5 ] 19-bp mosaic-end duplex = transposase binding site

library_generation.title: Transposition (tagmentation) of genomic DNA
library_generation.summary: Tn5 randomly fragments genomic DNA and tags both ends with the mosaic end + T7 loop.
library_generation.note: Transposition (55 C, 12 min) inserts the transposome, cutting genomic DNA into ~400-bp fragments. Because of transposome symmetry, both ends carry the same mosaic-end + single-stranded T7-promoter loop, with a 9-bp gap left by Tn5.
library_generation.product:
(ss T7 loop)                                        (ss T7 loop)
       \                                                  /
5'- AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[==== genomic fragment ====]         -3'
                       \___9-bp gap___/            \
3'-                    [==== genomic fragment ====]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT -5'

library_generation.title: Gap fill / extension (Q5 DNA polymerase)
library_generation.summary: Polymerase gap-extension converts the single-stranded T7 loops into double-stranded T7 promoters on both ends.
library_generation.note: Q5 DNA polymerase fills the 9-bp gaps and extends through the mosaic ends, making both ends double-stranded and creating functional double-stranded T7 promoters pointing inward on each end of the fragment.
library_generation.product:
5'-[T7 promoter=>]AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[==== genomic ====]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT[<=T7 promoter]-3'
3'-[<=T7 promoter]TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC[==== genomic ====]GACAGAGAATATGTGTGTAGA[T7 promoter=>]-5'
                 (double-stranded T7 promoter on BOTH ends)

library_generation.title: In vitro transcription (T7 RNA polymerase, overnight)
library_generation.summary: T7 linearly amplifies each fragment into thousands of copies of self-priming genomic RNA.
library_generation.note: Overnight T7 transcription (37 C, 10-16 h) makes single-stranded genomic RNA starting at the T7 +1 (GGG). Transcription is usually unidirectional (head-on collision suppresses the opposite promoter). The RNA 3' end forms a 27-nt self-priming hairpin from the converted mosaic-end sequence.
library_generation.product:
5'- GGGAGAUGUGUAUAAGAGACAG[==== genomic RNA ====]-3'   (RNA; 3' end self-primes)
                                              \__ 27-nt self-priming hairpin

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription (SuperScript IV)
library_generation.summary: The self-primed RNA 3' end is reverse-transcribed into first-strand cDNA.
library_generation.note: SuperScript IV extends from the self-priming 3' hairpin along the RNA template, generating first-strand cDNA copies of the genomic RNA.
library_generation.product:
5'- GGGAGAUGUGUAUAAGAGACAG[==== genomic RNA ====] -3'
3'- CCCUCU................[==== cDNA (1st strand) ====] <----------  -5'

library_generation.title: RNase digestion (RNase A + RNase H)
library_generation.summary: The RNA template is removed, leaving single-stranded first-strand cDNA.
library_generation.note: Affinity-purified RNase A and RNase H digest the RNA, leaving single-stranded cDNA whose 3' end carries the mosaic-end/T7-derived sequence.

library_generation.title: Second-strand synthesis with UMI primer
library_generation.summary: The UMI primer anneals to the cDNA 3' end and extends, forming double-stranded LIANTI amplicons tagged with an 8-bp UMI.
library_generation.note: The primer 5'-NNNNNNNNGGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-3' (Q5 polymerase) anneals to the 3' end of the cDNA and extends, adding a unique 8-bp molecular barcode to each amplicon. Amplicons from the same original fragment share the same 5' mapping coordinate but different UMIs, enabling digital fragment counting.

library_generation.title: Sonication (Covaris)
library_generation.summary: Double-stranded LIANTI amplicons are sheared to the insert size required for Illumina sequencing.
library_generation.note: LIANTI amplicons (~20 ng per cell) are sonicated to the length suitable for 2x125 bp / 2x150 bp reads; new genomic 3' ends are created, but the UMI-bearing 5' end (mosaic-end tag) is preserved for digital counting.
library_generation.product:
5'- [UMI:8]GGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[== genomic ==] ~~/~~ [== genomic ==] -3'
            (UMI-tagged 5' end kept)              sonicated genomic ends

library_generation.title: NEBNext library prep (end-repair, dA-tail, TruSeq adapter ligation, indexing PCR)
library_generation.summary: Sonicated amplicons are converted into a standard paired-end Illumina library with P5/P7 and an i7 index.
library_generation.note: NEBNext Ultra / Ultra II DNA Library Prep Kit performs end repair, dA-tailing, TruSeq forked-adapter ligation, and indexing PCR (size selection skipped). Reads at the UMI-tagged end capture the inline 8-bp UMI plus the 22-bp mosaic-end binding sequence before the genomic insert.

library_sequencing.note: Primer anneals to the bottom strand and reads the top strand rightward: first the inline 8-bp UMI, then the 22-bp T7 +1 (GGG) + Tn5 mosaic-end binding sequence, then the genomic insert. The UMI enables 5'-end digital fragment counting / duplicate removal (run as 2x125 or 2x150 bp).

library_sequencing.primer: Illumina TruSeq Read 2 / i7 index primer (5'-GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT-3')
library_sequencing.note: The i7 index read primer anneals to the top-strand Read 2 adapter and reads the 6-bp NEBNext sample index for demultiplexing.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'-...GTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT>revcomp aligns to top strand below
5'- ...AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCACNNNNNNATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
3'- ...TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTGNNNNNNTAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'
       primer 5'-GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT-3'------>  i7 index read
                                         |-i7-|

library_sequencing.note: Primer anneals to the top-strand Read 2 adapter and reads leftward through the genomic insert from the opposite (sonicated) amplicon end, giving the paired mate for mapping.

title: LIANTI
description: LIANTI (Linear Amplification via Transposon Insertion) is a single-cell whole-genome amplification (WGA) method that combines Tn5 transposition with T7 in vitro transcription to achieve linear — rather than exponential — amplification of a single cell's genomic DNA. A self-looping "pre-cut" transposon carrying a single-stranded T7-promoter loop tags genomic DNA; DNA-polymerase gap-fill converts the loops into double-stranded T7 promoters on both ends, and overnight in vitro transcription linearly amplifies each fragment into genomic RNA. The RNA self-primes at its 3' end, is reverse-transcribed, RNase-digested, and made double-stranded with a primer bearing an 8-bp unique molecular identifier (UMI), yielding LIANTI amplicons. Amplicons are sonicated and converted into a standard paired-end Illumina genomic library (NEBNext Ultra / TruSeq), where reads capture an inline 8-bp UMI plus the mosaic-end binding sequence on the 5' end of each amplicon, enabling digital fragment counting for high-accuracy micro-CNV and SNV detection.

reference.label: Single-cell whole-genome analyses by Linear Amplification via Transposon Insertion (LIANTI)

original_publication.title: Single-cell whole-genome analyses by Linear Amplification via Transposon Insertion (LIANTI)

throughput.summary: One single cell amplified per reaction (low-throughput; the authors note high-throughput multiplexing is possible by adding combinatorial cellular barcodes in the transposon and primer). ~6 pg single-cell genomic DNA yields ~20 ng of LIANTI amplicons per cell.
throughput.rna: not applicable (whole-genome DNA amplification; genomic RNA is only a linear-amplification intermediate)
throughput.dna: ~20 ng LIANTI amplicons from ~6 pg single-cell genomic DNA

publication.statistical_model: Poisson (read-depth CV compared against the Poisson sampling-noise lower limit); Extreme Value Distribution (EVD) used to set significance thresholds for CNV gain/loss calling

other.label: Cell line
other.value: BJ human diploid skin fibroblast (ATCC CRL-2522)

other.label: Micro-CNV resolution
other.value: ~10 kb via digital counting of inferred fragment numbers

other.label: Sequencing platforms
other.value: Illumina HiSeq 2500 (2x125/2x150) and HiSeq 4000 (2x150), skipping size selection

other.label: Data / toolkit
other.value: NCBI SRA SRP102259; 'lianti' toolkit at https://gitlab.com/lh3/lianti; mapping with BWA-MEM to GRCh37

other.label: Key application
other.value: Direct observation of stochastic replication-origin firing and genome-wide UV-induced SNV spectra (via kindred-cell pairs)

data_processing.summary: Reads are demultiplexed by the i7 sample index and the inline 8-bp UMI, aligned to the human reference (GRCh37) with BWA-MEM, and amplicons that map to identical start/end coordinates are collapsed to infer the original genomic-fragment number ('digital counting'); CNVs/micro-CNVs are called from binned fragment counts and read depth, and SNVs are filtered by comparing kindred-cell pairs. Analysis used the authors' own 'lianti' toolkit (gitlab.com/lh3/lianti).
data_processing.statistical_model: Poisson (read-depth sampling-noise reference for CV-vs-bin-size); Extreme Value Distribution (EVD) for CNV gain/loss significance thresholds
